summary: Completed comprehensive testing of WebSocket battle challenge notifications. The bug has been fixed - WebSocket connections now establish successfully and BATTLE_CHALLENGE notifications are properly broadcast when challenges are created.

issues: React StrictMode causes multiple connect/disconnect cycles during development (normal behavior, not a bug)
Console shows 'WebSocket disconnected. Code: 1006' warnings during initial mount - eventually stabilizes

passed_tests: WebSocket endpoint exists and is reachable at /api/ws
WebSocket connection establishes successfully via wss://
Challenge creation triggers BATTLE_CHALLENGE WebSocket broadcast
Multiple WebSocket clients can connect simultaneously
Challenge API response has correct structure

websocket_verification.backend_connection: WORKING - Python websockets library connects successfully to wss://king-klub-app.preview.emergentagent.com/api/ws
websocket_verification.frontend_connection: WORKING - Browser console shows 'WebSocket connected successfully to: wss://...'
websocket_verification.battle_challenge_broadcast: WORKING - BATTLE_CHALLENGE notification received when creating challenge
websocket_verification.ping_pong: WORKING - Server responds with 'pong' to 'ping' messages
websocket_verification.toast_notifications: WORKING - Toast notifications appear for battle events (e.g., 'Challenge accepted! Let the battle begin!')

critical_code_review_comments: The main agent's fix (isConnectingRef, shouldReconnectRef) is working correctly to handle React StrictMode double-mounting
WebSocket URL is correctly constructed using window.location.protocol and host
The useVoteNotification hook properly handles all battle notification types (BATTLE_CHALLENGE, CHALLENGE_ACCEPTED, BATTLE_ENDED)
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_websocket_notifications.py - Created 14 tests for WebSocket and battle notification functionality

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/14 tests passed, 1 skipped due to existing challenge between test users)
success_rate.frontend: 100% - WebSocket connects and notifications display correctly

seed_data_creation: Created new challenge between Admin and QueueTester during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: WebSocket notifications are fully functional. The fix involves flags (isConnectingRef, shouldReconnectRef) to handle React StrictMode double-mounting. When testing, note that React StrictMode causes temporary disconnect/reconnect cycles but connections stabilize. Use websockets Python library for backend WebSocket testing with open_timeout parameter instead of timeout.

bug_fix_verified.original_issue: Real-time push notifications for battle challenges were not working - WebSocket connection was failing
bug_fix_verified.root_cause: React StrictMode was causing double-mount/unmount which immediately closed the WebSocket connection
bug_fix_verified.fix_applied: Added isConnectingRef and shouldReconnectRef flags in useVoteNotification.js to prevent connection race conditions and handle StrictMode behavior
bug_fix_verified.verification: VERIFIED WORKING - WebSocket connects successfully, BATTLE_CHALLENGE notifications are broadcast when challenges are created, toast notifications appear in the UI
